changed_functions.name: MRxDAVQueryFileInformation
key_changes: FileStandardInformation (info class 5): NumberOfLinks changed from a read of FCB+0xE8 to the constant 1
FileStandardInformation: DeletePending changed from hardcoded 0 to *(FCB + 0x8C) & 1 (real FCB delete state)
changed_functions.security_notes: Information disclosure: the unpatched code reads NumberOfLinks from FCB offset 0xE8 and returns it to the caller in FILE_STANDARD_INFORMATION. That offset is not a maintained link count on the WebDAV FileStandardInformation path (the patch substitutes the constant 1), so a value that is not guaranteed initialized is disclosed to user mode. The DeletePending change from hardcoded 0 to the real FCB state is a correctness fix.
changed_functions.assembly_diff: UNPATCHED at 0x1C001EBD1: mov eax,[rsi+0E8h]; 0x1C001EBD7 mov [rdi+10h],eax; 0x1C001EBCA mov [rdi+14h],bl (bl=0)  ->  PATCHED at 0x1C001EBFC: mov ecx,1; 0x1C001EC19 mov al,[rsi+8Ch]; 0x1C001EC1F and al,cl; 0x1C001EC21 mov [rdi+10h],ecx; 0x1C001EC28 mov [rdi+14h],al

changed_functions.name: DavReadWriteFileEx
key_changes: Function signature gains a new int32 parameter (the caching flag)
changed_functions.security_notes: The unpatched DavReadWriteFileEx unconditionally sets bit 0 (IRP_NOCACHE) of the IRP Flags field. The patch makes it conditional on a caller-supplied flag. This is part of a caching-consistency correctness change; no security impact is demonstrated by the diff.
changed_functions.assembly_diff: UNPATCHED at 0x1C001BA57: or dword [rsi+10h], 1  ->  PATCHED at 0x1C001BAA8: mov eax,[rsp+arg_48]; 0x1C001BAAF and eax,1; 0x1C001BAB2 or [r14+10h],eax

changed_functions.name: MRxDAVCreate
key_changes: PATCHED adds RxDisableLocalBuffering(*(RX_CONTEXT + 0x38)) at 0x1C0010753 (absent in unpatched)
RxDisableLocalBuffering is an RDBSS import present only in the patched binary
changed_functions.security_notes: The unpatched driver never calls RxDisableLocalBuffering when creating WebDAV file objects, so RDBSS leaves local buffering enabled. The patch disables it, part of the caching-consistency change. No security impact is demonstrated by the diff.
changed_functions.assembly_diff: Patched adds call cs:__imp_RxDisableLocalBuffering at 0x1C0010753 with rcx = *(RX_CONTEXT + 0x38); in the .c: RxDisableLocalBuffering(*(_QWORD *)(a1 + 56))

changed_functions.name: MRxDAVCreateContinuation
changed_functions.security_notes: The unpatched code ORs FILE_NO_INTERMEDIATE_BUFFERING (0x8) into CreateOptions when opening the local backing file via ZwCreateFile. The patch removes the forced flag. Part of the caching-consistency change; no security impact demonstrated.
changed_functions.assembly_diff: UNPATCHED: or r8d, 8 at 0x1C0012AB1 (r8d = *(arg2 + 0x20C)) before ZwCreateFile at 0x1C0012B08  ->  PATCHED: no such OR before ZwCreateFile at 0x1C0012B12

changed_functions.name: MRxDAVReadContinuation
key_changes: All DavReadWriteFileEx call sites pass the new caching flag *(*(RX_CONTEXT + 0x28) + 0x10)
Stack frame increased from 0x88 to 0x98 for the additional parameter
changed_functions.security_notes: Threads the caching flag into DavReadWriteFileEx so IRP_NOCACHE is set conditionally. Part of the caching-consistency change; no security impact demonstrated. Register reallocation is a side effect of the added parameter.
changed_functions.assembly_diff: sub rsp, 0x88 -> sub rsp, 0x98; flag loaded via mov rax,[rdi+28h] then mov eax,[rax+10h] (0x1C001AC36/0x1C001AC42) and passed to DavReadWriteFileEx

changed_functions.name: MRxDAVWriteContinuation
key_changes: All DavReadWriteFileEx call sites pass the new caching flag *(*(RX_CONTEXT + 0x28) + 0x10)
changed_functions.security_notes: Same caching-consistency change as MRxDAVReadContinuation on the write path. No security impact demonstrated.

security_findings.entry_point: NtQueryInformationFile / GetFileInformationByHandle with FileStandardInformation (info class 5) on a WebDAV file handle
call_chain: FileStandardInformation branch (loc_1C001EB6F) reads FCB+0xE8 as NumberOfLinks
security_findings.vulnerability_class: Use of uninitialized resource (CWE-908) / Information exposure (CWE-200)
security_findings.description: When handling FileStandardInformation (info class 5), the unpatched MRxDAVQueryFileInformation copies the DWORD at FCB offset 0xE8 into the NumberOfLinks field of the caller's FILE_STANDARD_INFORMATION and returns it to user mode. That offset does not hold a maintained link count on the WebDAV path; the patch replaces the read with the constant 1, indicating the source field was not a valid, initialized value. The 4-byte disclosure is returned once per query. Separately, the unpatched code hardcodes DeletePending to 0; the patch reads the real state from FCB+0x8C bit 0 (a correctness fix).
security_findings.trigger_conditions: 1. Open a file via the WebDAV redirector (e.g. CreateFile on a UNC path like \\server\DavWWWRoot\file). 2. Call NtQueryInformationFile (or GetFileInformationByHandle) with FileStandardInformation on the handle. 3. The returned FILE_STANDARD_INFORMATION.NumberOfLinks contains the value at FCB+0xE8 under the unpatched driver, or the constant 1 under the patched driver.
security_findings.primitive: Disclosure of the 4-byte value at FCB+0xE8 in the NumberOfLinks field, once per query. The diff does not establish what those bytes contain, so no further claim about the disclosed contents or downstream exploitation is made.
security_findings.assembly:
; UNPATCHED MRxDAVQueryFileInformation - FileStandardInformation success path (loc_1C001EB6F)
; rsi = FCB, rdi = output buffer
0x1C001EBA9: mov eax, [rsi+18h]      ; AllocationSize.LowPart
0x1C001EBAC: and r15b, 10h           ; Directory/reparse flag
0x1C001EBB0: mov [rdi], eax
0x1C001EBB2: mov r14d, 18h           ; output size = 24
0x1C001EBB8: mov eax, [rsi+1Ch]      ; AllocationSize.HighPart
0x1C001EBBB: mov [rdi+4], eax
0x1C001EBBE: mov eax, [rsi+20h]      ; EndOfFile.LowPart
0x1C001EBC1: mov [rdi+8], eax
0x1C001EBC4: mov eax, [rsi+24h]      ; EndOfFile.HighPart
0x1C001EBC7: mov [rdi+0Ch], eax
0x1C001EBCA: mov [rdi+14h], bl       ; DeletePending = 0 (bl zeroed at entry)
0x1C001EBCD: mov [rdi+15h], r15b     ; Directory/reparse
0x1C001EBD1: mov eax, [rsi+0E8h]     ; reads FCB+0xE8 (not a link count)
0x1C001EBD7: mov [rdi+10h], eax      ; -> NumberOfLinks returned to user
0x1C001EBDA: jmp loc_1C001EC80

; PATCHED (loc_1C001EBF9)
0x1C001EBFC: mov ecx, 1              ; NumberOfLinks = 1
0x1C001EC19: mov al, [rsi+8Ch]       ; real DeletePending state
0x1C001EC1F: and al, cl              ; mask bit 0
0x1C001EC21: mov [rdi+10h], ecx      ; -> NumberOfLinks = 1
0x1C001EC28: mov [rdi+14h], al       ; -> DeletePending real state

breakpoints.why: Entry of the function (unpatched). arg1 (rcx) is the RX_CONTEXT. FCB = *(rcx+0x38); info class = *(rcx+0x1b0) (must be 5); output buffer = *(rcx+0x1b8); output length = *(rcx+0x1c8) (must be >= 0x18).
The instruction 'mov eax,[rsi+0E8h]' that reads FCB+0xE8 as NumberOfLinks (unpatched). rsi = FCB; dd rsi+0xe8 L1 is the value returned. Removed by the patch.

watch: rsi at 0x1C001EBD1 = FCB pointer; [rsi+0xe8] is the value returned as NumberOfLinks
rdi at 0x1C001EBD1 = output FILE_STANDARD_INFORMATION buffer; [rdi+0x10] receives the value after 0x1C001EBD7
*(RX_CONTEXT + 0x1b0) = file information class; must equal 5 to reach this path
key_offsets: 0x1C001EB6F: FileStandardInformation branch, cmp ebp,18h size check
0x1C001EBD1: mov eax,[rsi+0E8h] -- read of FCB+0xE8 (unpatched, removed by patch)
debugging_aids.trigger_setup: 1. Ensure the WebClient service is running. 2. Make a WebDAV file reachable via UNC. 3. HANDLE h = CreateFileW(L"\\\\server\\DavWWWRoot\\file.txt", GENERIC_READ, FILE_SHARE_READ, NULL, OPEN_EXISTING, 0, NULL); 4. FILE_STANDARD_INFORMATION fsi = {0}; IO_STATUS_BLOCK iosb = {0}; NtQueryInformationFile(h, &iosb, &fsi, sizeof(fsi), FileStandardInformation); 5. Read fsi.NumberOfLinks.
debugging_aids.expected_observation: Under the unpatched binary, fsi.NumberOfLinks reflects the value at FCB+0xE8. Under the patched binary it is the constant 1. The unpatched DeletePending field is always 0 regardless of the file's actual delete state.
debugging_aids.notes: FILE_STANDARD_INFORMATION layout: +0x00 AllocationSize(8), +0x08 EndOfFile(8), +0x10 NumberOfLinks(4), +0x14 DeletePending(1), +0x15 Directory(1). The FCB pointer comes from *(RX_CONTEXT + 0x38). The diff does not establish what FCB+0xE8 contains at query time.

security_findings.entry_point: ReadFile / WriteFile on a WebDAV handle -> MRxDAVRead/MRxDAVWrite -> MRxDAVReadContinuation/MRxDAVWriteContinuation -> DavReadWriteFileEx
security_findings.description: The unpatched driver combines three behaviors the patch makes consistent: MRxDAVCreate never calls RxDisableLocalBuffering (RDBSS local buffering stays enabled); MRxDAVCreateContinuation opens the backing local file with FILE_NO_INTERMEDIATE_BUFFERING; and DavReadWriteFileEx unconditionally sets IRP_NOCACHE. The patch adds RxDisableLocalBuffering, drops FILE_NO_INTERMEDIATE_BUFFERING, and makes IRP_NOCACHE conditional on a caller-supplied flag threaded through the read/write continuations. This is a caching-consistency correctness change. The binary diff does not demonstrate any stale-read, data-corruption, or cross-user disclosure condition, so no vulnerability class or exploit primitive is asserted.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: No attacker-triggerable condition is demonstrated by the diff. The change alters cached vs. uncached I/O on the backing file and whether RDBSS local buffering is disabled.
security_findings.assembly:
; UNPATCHED DavReadWriteFileEx (entry 0x1C001B7C8)
0x1C001BA57: or dword ptr [rsi+10h], 1   ; IRP->Flags |= IRP_NOCACHE (unconditional)

; PATCHED DavReadWriteFileEx (entry 0x1C001B818)
0x1C001BAA8: mov eax, [rsp+arg_48]        ; caller-supplied caching flag (a10)
0x1C001BAAF: and eax, 1                    ; mask bit 0
0x1C001BAB2: or [r14+10h], eax             ; IRP->Flags |= (flag & 1)

; MRxDAVCreate: patched adds call cs:__imp_RxDisableLocalBuffering at 0x1C0010753
; MRxDAVCreateContinuation: unpatched 'or r8d, 8' at 0x1C0012AB1 (r8d = *(arg2+0x20C)) removed in patched

breakpoints.why: Entry of the function that sets IRP_NOCACHE (unpatched; patched entry 0x1C001B818).

The instruction 'or dword [rsi+10h], 1' that unconditionally sets IRP_NOCACHE (unpatched). rsi = allocated IRP. In the patched build the equivalent OR at 0x1C001BAB2 uses the caller-supplied flag loaded at 0x1C001BAA8.
The RxDisableLocalBuffering call at 0x1C0010753 exists only in the patched build.

watch: rsi at 0x1C001BA57 = allocated IRP; [rsi+0x10] = IRP.Flags; bit 0 is set unconditionally in the unpatched build
In MRxDAVCreate, the RxDisableLocalBuffering import is present only in the patched binary
debugging_aids.trigger_setup: Not applicable; no attacker-triggerable security condition is demonstrated by the diff.
debugging_aids.expected_observation: At 0x1C001BA57 (unpatched) IRP.Flags bit 0 is set unconditionally; the patched build gates the same OR on the caller-supplied flag loaded from the stack.
debugging_aids.notes: The new parameter to DavReadWriteFileEx is sourced from *(*(RX_CONTEXT + 0x28) + 0x10). The change spans five functions: MRxDAVCreate (adds RxDisableLocalBuffering), MRxDAVCreateContinuation (removes FILE_NO_INTERMEDIATE_BUFFERING), DavReadWriteFileEx (conditional NOCACHE), MRxDAVReadContinuation and MRxDAVWriteContinuation (thread the flag).

summary: The substantive security fix in this patch is a kernel information disclosure in MRxDAVQueryFileInformation: when handling FileStandardInformation queries, the unpatched code reads NumberOfLinks from FCB offset 0xE8 and returns it to the caller. That offset is not a maintained link count on the WebDAV path (the patch substitutes the constant 1), so a value that is not guaranteed initialized is disclosed to user mode, once per query (CWE-908). The patch also fixes DeletePending, which was hardcoded to 0, to read the real FCB state. What the disclosed 4 bytes contain is not established by the diff, so no further claim about the disclosed contents is made. The other five changed functions form a caching-consistency correctness change (add RxDisableLocalBuffering, drop FILE_NO_INTERMEDIATE_BUFFERING, make IRP_NOCACHE conditional); the diff does not demonstrate any stale-read or cross-user condition, so that change is not rated as a vulnerability. An independent per-function comparison of the two builds confirms exactly these six functions differ substantively; all other differences are relocation and WPP trace-GUID label churn.
